Name:
Me Or Us [Vinyl Version]
(feat. Travis Scott) (prod. Rex Kudo, Post Malone & Charlie Handsome)
Notes: Updated version of "Me or Us" with a Travis Scott feature that only released on the vinyl version of the album. The week after release producer Charlie Handsome spoke about the feature, saying that the plan was to re-upload the song to streaming with the new verse, similar to how "Floyd Mayweather" was updated and how "You Said" eventually would be as well. For reasons never given, this update never came on streaming, though it's speculated that Travis' verse wasn't ever actually finished since one included on the vinyl contains a fair amount of mumble vocals, though it's unclear why it would be included on the vinyl if that was the case. Charlie Handsome also mentioned that Thug originally wanted Frank Ocean to be added, but scheduling issues prevented this.

While Thug & Juice had previously previewed the song with just each other on it, in August 2019, Nicki teased the song until the first chorus while previewing music from her new album, hinting she had potentially been recording for it. Asian Da Brat also recorded a song on this same beat, but she later swapped out the beat for the final version of that song, “Big Racks”. Thug's vocals recorded by Alex Tumay and Flo. Ongonga, Juice's by Max Lord, and Nicki's by Aubry “Big Juice” Delaine. Mixed by Bainz, Aresh Banaji, Max Lord and Aubry “Big Juice” Delaine.

Track 12 on Lil Baby's album "The Leaks". Thug's verses is heavily themed around his RICO trial and conflicts it caused between him and other YSL assosciates. The back half of Thug's verse was rerecorded and a line from Lil Baby was censored before release, presumably due to legal concerns relating to his probation. Mastered by Colin Leonard.
